content:
🌟 Profanity-Filtering-All-languages | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To filter out or censor profane language in text across a wide range of languages for applications like chat platforms, content moderation, or user-generated content.
✨ Key Features: • 107 languages support • Blazing fast O(1) lookups • Lazy loading of locale data • Zero runtime dependencies • Rich API (check, detect, censor, addWords, removeWords)
📖 Summary: This TypeScript library provides a highly performant profanity filtering solution supporting 107 languages. It offers features like lazy loading, zero runtime dependencies, and a rich API for checking, detecting, and censoring profanity, with options for customization.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ clamshell | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling laptop users to close their lid and use external monitors seamlessly on Linux, regardless of power connection or init system.
✨ Key Features: • Implements clamshell mode on Linux • Does not require AC power • Independent of init systems • Configurable suspend modes (freeze, suspend_to_ram, suspend_to_disk) • Specific NVIDIA GPU suspend handling
📖 Summary: Clamshell is a Linux daemon that enables clamshell mode, allowing users to close their laptop lid and continue working with external displays and peripherals without requiring AC power or a specific init system. It offers flexible suspend mode options and specific handling for NVIDIA GPUs.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 sshb | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a fun, interactive Tamagotchi-like experience within the user's terminal environment, enhancing engagement with command-line usage.
✨ Key Features: • Tamagotchi-style virtual pet in the terminal • Persistent pet state • ASCII art pet with animated expressions • Four core stats: Hunger, Energy, Happiness, Health • Background daemon for stat decay (systemd)
📖 Summary: SSHB (SSH Buddy) is a terminal-based Tamagotchi-style virtual pet that lives in your command line. It requires regular care, with its stats decaying over time, and can be integrated into your bash prompt to display its status.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 kugelaudio-open | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Providing high-quality, open-source text-to-speech synthesis for a wide range of European languages, enabling developers and researchers to create natural-sounding speech for various applications.
✨ Key Features: • Open-source text-to-speech for European languages • AR + Diffusion architecture • High-quality voice cloning capabilities • Outperforms leading commercial TTS systems (e.g., ElevenLabs) in human preference benchmarks
📖 Summary: KugelAudio is an open-source text-to-speech (TTS) system focused on European languages, leveraging an AR + Diffusion architecture. It aims to bridge the gap in high-quality TTS for underserved European languages, offering performance that rivals and surpasses leading commercial solutions.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ dangerzone | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To safely convert untrusted documents into a secure PDF format, mitigating potential security risks from malicious content.
✨ Key Features: • Sandboxed document conversion • Conversion to pixel-based PDFs • Optional OCR for text layer • PDF compression • Support for various document and image formats
📖 Summary: Dangerzone is a security tool that converts potentially dangerous documents (PDFs, Office files, images) into safe, pixel-based PDFs. It achieves this by processing the documents within a sandboxed environment, converting them to raw pixel data, and then reconstructing them as new PDFs, thus neutralizing embedded threats.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
